vulnerabilities.title: Rendertron discloses absolute paths of files
vulnerabilities.description: Installed packages are exposed by node_modules in Rendertron 1.0.0, allowing remote attackers to read absolute paths on the server by examining the "_where" attribute of package.json files.

vulnerabilities.title: rendertron XSS vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: Error reporting within Rendertron 1.0.0 allows reflected Cross Site Scripting (XSS) from invalid URLs.

vulnerabilities.title: rendertron LFI vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: Rendertron 1.0.0 allows for alternative protocols such as 'file://' introducing a Local File Inclusion (LFI) bug where arbitrary files can be read by a remote attacker.

vulnerabilities.title: rendertron can remotely shut down Chrome instance
vulnerabilities.description: Rendertron 1.0.0 includes an `_ah/stop` route to shutdown the Chrome instance responsible for serving render requests to all users. Visiting this route with a GET request allows any unauthorized remote attacker to disable the core service of the application.

vulnerabilities.title: SSRF in Rendertron
vulnerabilities.description: Rendertron versions prior to 3.0.0 are are susceptible to a Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) attack. An attacker can use a specially crafted webpage to force a rendertron headless chrome process to render internal sites it has access to, and display it as a screenshot. Suggested mitigations are to upgrade your rendertron to version 3.0.0, or, if you cannot update, to secure the infrastructure to limit the headless chrome's access to your internal domain.
